fail_to_pass.name: empty_`allowedhosts:_[]`_is_rejected_with_an_error_message_t
fail_to_pass.description:
Empty `allowedHosts: []` is rejected with an error message that
mentions `allowedHosts`. Either at construction (preferred) or on the
first request that exercises the dynamic resolver.

fail_to_pass.name: a_request_bearing_`x-forwarded-host`_matching_an_allowed_hos
fail_to_pass.description:
A request bearing `x-forwarded-host` matching an allowed host resolves
the per-request baseURL to that host, with `x-forwarded-proto`-derived
scheme. The middleware-visible `ctx.context.baseURL` reflects the
resolved value.

fail_to_pass.name: a_request_from_a_host_outside_`allowedhosts`_(with_no_`fallb
fail_to_pass.description:
A request from a host outside `allowedHosts` (with no `fallback`
configured) causes `auth.handler(req)` to reject — the server
refuses to operate under an untrusted host.

fail_to_pass.name: wildcard_host_patterns_(e.g._`*.vercel.app`,_`preview-*.myap
fail_to_pass.description:
Wildcard host patterns (e.g. `*.vercel.app`, `preview-*.myapp.com`)
match hosts as expected. A `*.vercel.app` allows any subdomain, and a
prefix pattern `preview-*.myapp.com` matches preview-named subdomains.

fail_to_pass.name: when_the_request's_host_is_not_in_`allowedhosts`_but_a_`fall
fail_to_pass.description:
When the request's host is not in `allowedHosts` but a `fallback`
URL is configured, the resolver uses the fallback (instead of
throwing). The fallback is the configured escape hatch.

fail_to_pass.name:
the_optional_`protocol`_config_takes_precedence_over
`x-forw
fail_to_pass.description:
The optional `protocol` config takes precedence over
`x-forwarded-proto`. When `protocol: "https"` is set, the resolved
scheme is always https, even if the proxy header says `http`.

fail_to_pass.name: `trustedorigins`_is_auto-expanded_from_`allowedhosts`_so_wil
fail_to_pass.description:
`trustedOrigins` is auto-expanded from `allowedHosts` so wildcard
hosts and fallback origins are CSRF-trusted by default. Localhost
entries get both http:// and https:// variants since local dev
typically uses http.

fail_to_pass.name: with_`crosssubdomaincookies.enabled:_true`_and_a_dynamic_bas
fail_to_pass.description:
With `crossSubDomainCookies.enabled: true` and a dynamic baseURL,
the cookie's `Domain=` attribute is recomputed per-request to match
the resolved host. Otherwise multi-domain deployments cannot share
cookies correctly per tenant.

fail_to_pass.name: the_public_option_type_for_`baseurl`_accepts_both_a_static_s
fail_to_pass.description:
The public option type for `baseURL` accepts both a static string and
a dynamic config object `{ allowedHosts: string[], fallback?: string,
protocol?: "http" | "https" | "auto" }`. The dynamic shape is named
and exported from the public types so application authors can refer
to it.

fail_to_pass.name:
when_two_requests_for_distinct_allowed_hosts_are_processed
c
fail_to_pass.description:
When two requests for distinct allowed hosts are processed
concurrently, each request's resolved baseURL / cookie domain /
trustedOrigins is isolated — no cross-contamination from a shared
mutated AuthContext. Without this, the multi-tenant case is broken
under any non-trivial load.

fail_to_pass.name: pre-existing_consumers_of_`options.baseurl`_that_previously
fail_to_pass.description:
Pre-existing consumers of `options.baseURL` that previously assumed a
string (cookies, jwt sign/verify, mcp, oauth-proxy, oidc-provider,
passkey, telemetry, svelte-kit) now safely handle the wider type —
neither crash nor stringify-coerce the object. Workspace-wide
TypeScript build still passes after the option type widening.

pass_to_pass.name: static-string_baseurl_still_works_after_the_option_type_wide
pass_to_pass.description:
Static-string baseURL still works after the option type widening — a
regression guard for apps that pass `baseURL: "http://localhost:3000"`
(string).
